events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: residential HVAC-R, furnace & venting, in-progress, observed: PVC flue slope back-pitched (condensing). Need identify hazards severity immediate controls. Use references IRC M1801, NFPA 54. Need decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Back-pitched condensing flue prevents drainage of condensate, can cause corrosion, blockage, flue gas spillage, CO, furnace failure. Severity high maybe critical due to CO poisoning potential. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 60? value not relevant. sound_source etc default empty string/zero/list. Need produce valid JSON with all fields. Need include findings, actions, rationale, references. Ensure no prose outside JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 60,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "PVC flue is back-pitched, preventing condensate drainage in a condensing appliance vent.",
    "Standing condensate can block flue gases, corrode the vent, and cause combustion products to spill into the living space.",
    "Condition violates required positive slope to drain condensate back to the appliance or condensate trap."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down and lock out/tag out the furnace until the vent is corrected.",
    "Re-pitch the PVC flue so it drains continuously toward the appliance or approved condensate drain point per manufacturer instructions.",
    "Inspect and clear any accumulated condensate from the vent and combustion/condensate trap.",
    "Verify all vent joints are properly supported, sealed, and sloped before returning to service.",
    "Perform combustion analysis and spillage/CO test after correction."
  ],
  "rationale": "A condensing furnace produces corrosive condensate in the flue. Back-pitching traps liquid, obstructs vent flow, risks heat exchanger and vent failure, and can cause carbon monoxide spillage. This is an immediate safety and code compliance issue.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801 - Vent systems",
    "NFPA 54 - National Fuel Gas Code, venting of gas appliances"
  ]
}
